nodes.rationale: 4 broad US + 11 SPDR sectors + 14 country/regional, each with verified first-bar date and inclusion reason; frozen 2026-07-08 so universe changes require a new dated decision.

nodes.label: E9 — 'never book a loss' deep-dip audit (FAIL; claim literally true but bad)
nodes.rationale: The Reddit claim's 100% win rate (0/53 realized losses) is confirmed AND bad: gate CAGR 3.46%, a −79.7% unrealized position, ~17-year underwater hold — the win rate measures bookkeeping, not performance.
nodes.norm_label: e9 — 'never book a loss' deep-dip audit (fail; claim literally true but bad)

nodes.label: Scope guard: Trading repo/DB read-only, paper only, no unauthorized strategy surface
nodes.rationale: Decided by Evan 2026-07-08: never modify D:\ClaudeCode\Trading from this project; price_cache read-only; no concurrent backtests; paper trading only; if a task requires breaking these, stop and report.
nodes.norm_label: scope guard: trading repo/db read-only, paper only, no unauthorized strategy surface

nodes.label: Pre-registration discipline (rules committed before results code)
nodes.rationale: Git history must prove the prereg doc commit predates any backtest-engine commit; thresholds provably precede results, so 'it passed' is verifiable, not asserted. A FAIL is a valid publishable outcome; no tuning past a pre-registered kill.
nodes.norm_label: pre-registration discipline (rules committed before results code)

nodes.label: Frozen-regression tripwire (swing_bot/test_frozen, d=±0.0000pp)
nodes.rationale: Ported from Trading's pattern so an unrelated code change can't silently alter a pinned past result; 12 references pinned at ±0.0000pp.

nodes.label: LLM cash-veto overlay experiment (control + treatment sleeves)

nodes.norm_label: llm cash-veto overlay experiment (control + treatment sleeves)

nodes.label: Overnight-gap execution haircut (54% of IBS edge in close→next-open gap)
nodes.rationale: Explains the mean-reversion family's failure: over half the published IBS edge lives in the overnight gap an EOD bot forfeits, and what remains does not survive transaction costs; the non-executable close-to-close variant would have passed.
nodes.norm_label: overnight-gap execution haircut (54% of ibs edge in close→next-open gap)

nodes.norm_label: prereg e1 — etf ibs mean reversion (commit 8963e49)

nodes.label: E1 — ETF IBS mean reversion, 29 ETFs (FAIL: Sharpe 0.23, maxDD 36%)
nodes.norm_label: e1 — etf ibs mean reversion, 29 etfs (fail: sharpe 0.23, maxdd 36%)

nodes.label: IBS indicator: (close − low) / (high − low), entry <0.20, exit >0.80 or 5-day time stop
nodes.rationale: Parameters are literature/convention-set (Pagonidis IBS thresholds, Connors-style time stop), NOT fitted to project data — so full-sample evaluation is valid. No hard stop-loss: literature indicates fixed stops structurally hurt mean reversion.
nodes.norm_label: ibs indicator: (close − low) / (high − low), entry <0.20, exit >0.80 or 5-day time stop

nodes.label: Kill criteria: conjunction of pre-registered PASS thresholds (N, expectancy, Sharpe/CAGR, maxDD)
nodes.rationale: 'Survives' is a conjunction of fixed thresholds; any miss = FAIL, recorded honestly with no parameter tuning to rescue it — a changed rule requires a new dated pre-registration.
nodes.norm_label: kill criteria: conjunction of pre-registered pass thresholds (n, expectancy, sharpe/cagr, maxdd)

nodes.label: Prereg E1b — broad-US IBS out-of-sample test (commit 0126ce3)
nodes.norm_label: prereg e1b — broad-us ibs out-of-sample test (commit 0126ce3)

nodes.label: E1b — IBS on SPY/QQQ/DIA/IWM holdout 2022–26 (FAIL near-miss: Sharpe 0.4961 vs 0.50)
nodes.rationale: The broad_us subset was chosen after seeing E1 results, so its full-sample number is contaminated; the only clean test is the unseen 2022–2026 holdout. Redefining a failed experiment around a data-suggested subset was refused as p-hacking and spun into its own pre-registration instead.
nodes.norm_label: e1b — ibs on spy/qqq/dia/iwm holdout 2022–26 (fail near-miss: sharpe 0.4961 vs 0.50)

nodes.label: E2 — IBS on 3x ETFs, K=2 (FAIL: holdout CAGR 7.98% vs 15%, maxDD 60.6% vs 60%; IBS family shelved)
nodes.rationale: Pre-committed multiplicity guard (§7): if E2 fails, the whole IBS family — 1x and leveraged — is SHELVED with no E2b/E1c/cost-shaved re-runs; third and last pre-registered test of the family.
nodes.norm_label: e2 — ibs on 3x etfs, k=2 (fail: holdout cagr 7.98% vs 15%, maxdd 60.6% vs 60%; ibs family shelved)

nodes.label: E3 — concentrated stock momentum, top-3 of ~39 survivor large-caps (FAIL clean: 6.27% CAGR vs 15%; lost to buy-hold)
nodes.norm_label: e3 — concentrated stock momentum, top-3 of ~39 survivor large-caps (fail clean: 6.27% cagr vs 15%; lost to buy-hold)

nodes.label: Asymmetric-falsification design (only a FAIL is interpretable)
nodes.rationale: Survivorship and lookahead bias both inflate a stock backtest and cannot be removed with available data — so run on a deliberately bias-flattered survivor universe and treat only a FAILURE as meaningful; a PASS is uninterpretable and routes to forward live paper.
nodes.norm_label: asymmetric-falsification design (only a fail is interpretable)

nodes.label: E4 — 3x MA rotation (PASS 2014–26, CAGR 33.8%; later killed by E5 regime test)
nodes.rationale: The primary cell was already seen in the B4 screen (contaminated), so E4's kill criteria target what was UNSEEN: fragility across a 20-cell MA-length/lag/cost grid, and whether timing adds value over buy-and-hold benchmarks.
nodes.norm_label: e4 — 3x ma rotation (pass 2014–26, cagr 33.8%; later killed by e5 regime test)

nodes.label: 200-day-MA rotation rule (hold fund while signal close > SMA(200), else cash; switch next open)
nodes.norm_label: 200-day-ma rotation rule (hold fund while signal close > sma(200), else cash; switch next open)

nodes.label: Prereg E5 — E4 rotation across hostile regimes 2000–13 (commit 09a3a31)
nodes.norm_label: prereg e5 — e4 rotation across hostile regimes 2000–13 (commit 09a3a31)

nodes.label: E5 — regime test of E4 on 2000–2013 (FAIL: −3.4% CAGR, 92.7% drawdown → E4 regime-dependent, de-authorized)
nodes.rationale: Attacks E4's biggest weakness — that its return is 3x Nasdaq over the best tech decade — by reconstructing the dot-com and 2008 regimes TQQQ never lived through; in choppy secular bears the rule re-enters 3x right before the next leg down and leverage turns whipsaw into ruin.
nodes.norm_label: e5 — regime test of e4 on 2000–2013 (fail: −3.4% cagr, 92.7% drawdown → e4 regime-dependent, de-authorized)

nodes.label: Synthetic daily-rebalanced 3x Nasdaq series (3x daily QQQ returns minus calibrated drag)
nodes.rationale: Daily rebalancing means 3x of DAILY returns, not cumulative; drag calibrated so 2014–26 CAGR matches real TQQQ (validated: correlation 0.9989, drag ~4%/yr). Caveat pinned a priori: pre-2014 financing costs understated, biasing mildly against the rotation's relative case only.
nodes.norm_label: synthetic daily-rebalanced 3x nasdaq series (3x daily qqq returns minus calibrated drag)

nodes.label: Prereg E6 — de-leveraged 1x 200-MA rotation as drawdown overlay (commit 0526ea2)
nodes.norm_label: prereg e6 — de-leveraged 1x 200-ma rotation as drawdown overlay (commit 0526ea2)

nodes.label: E6 — 1x MA rotation on QQQ (PASS as risk-mgmt overlay: cuts maxDD 83%→52%, return ≈ buy-hold; later downgraded to market-dependent by E7)
nodes.rationale: Explicitly NOT the high-return goal: tests the humbler claim that 200-MA timing improves risk-adjusted return in every regime. 2000–2013 was already seen at 3x in E5, so E6 is confirmatory-on-seen-data, not clean OOS.
nodes.norm_label: e6 — 1x ma rotation on qqq (pass as risk-mgmt overlay: cuts maxdd 83%→52%, return ≈ buy-hold; later downgraded to market-dependent by e7)

nodes.label: Prereg E7 — international validation on unseen regimes (commit 70ed2a1)
nodes.norm_label: prereg e7 — international validation on unseen regimes (commit 70ed2a1)

nodes.label: E7 — international out-of-sample: Nikkei/DAX/FTSE/HSI/ASX (both arms FAIL; high-return-robust question closed)
nodes.rationale: US backtest regimes were exhausted (both crash regimes already used to judge the rotation family), so five non-US indices back to 1985 — above all the 1990s Japan secular bear — supply genuinely unseen independent regimes; the honest way to test without live paper. HSI 3x was mathematically wiped to zero by the single-day 1987 crash.
nodes.norm_label: e7 — international out-of-sample: nikkei/dax/ftse/hsi/asx (both arms fail; high-return-robust question closed)

nodes.label: A-priori vol-gated 3x rotation (hold 3x only if close > SMA200 AND 20d vol < 30%)
nodes.rationale: The 30% threshold is fixed from first principles (3x on a 30%-vol index runs ~90% position vol, the prudence ceiling) and the 5%/yr drag set conservatively — NOT fit to any observed crash, so the non-US data is a true first test.
nodes.norm_label: a-priori vol-gated 3x rotation (hold 3x only if close > sma200 and 20d vol < 30%)

nodes.norm_label: swing.db — project sqlite (bars table pk ticker,date; positions/nav/results)

nodes.label: Own yfinance fetcher (swing_bot/prices.py, auto_adjust=False) — M0.2 data-path decision
nodes.rationale: Chosen over reusing Trading's price_cache because price_cache stores only close+volume+flags — no high/low/open — so IBS is uncomputable from it; it also lacks DIA/IWM and all country ETFs and has zero next_open rows for ETFs.
nodes.norm_label: own yfinance fetcher (swing_bot/prices.py, auto_adjust=false) — m0.2 data-path decision

nodes.label: conventions bin — doc system + code conventions
nodes.norm_label: conventions bin — doc system + code conventions

nodes.norm_label: features bin (empty at bootstrap — no code yet)

nodes.label: gotchas bin — data traps inherited from Trading + measured local traps
nodes.norm_label: gotchas bin — data traps inherited from trading + measured local traps

nodes.label: Survivorship bias — yfinance carries currently-listed names only; every backtest upper-bound-biased
nodes.rationale: Short-horizon mean reversion is worst hit — delisted crashers are exactly what an RSI<30 buyer catches. No fix exists in the current stack; must be stated in every result.
nodes.norm_label: survivorship bias — yfinance carries currently-listed names only; every backtest upper-bound-biased

nodes.label: Price convention: SPLIT-ADJUSTED, DIVIDEND-UNADJUSTED (auto_adjust=False)
nodes.rationale: Every consumer must honor it or returns are silently wrong; every price-touching script states it in a header comment.

nodes.label: XLRE zero-range bars (19 bars with high==low, 2015-10..2016-02) — IBS divides by zero
nodes.rationale: Illiquid early post-launch trading, not a split error; signal code must skip any ticker on a high==low day (IBS undefined → no signal), not crash.
nodes.norm_label: xlre zero-range bars (19 bars with high==low, 2015-10..2016-02) — ibs divides by zero

nodes.label: Engine v1 sizing gotcha: fixed initial-capital/K, not current-NAV/K (implicit leverage after losses)
nodes.rationale: At K=1 on 3x funds NAV crossed zero (maxDD 104%); immaterial for diversified 1x runs and does not invalidate pinned refs (all experiments shared these semantics), but any future engine or live loop must size on current NAV.
nodes.norm_label: engine v1 sizing gotcha: fixed initial-capital/k, not current-nav/k (implicit leverage after losses)

nodes.label: security bin — API-key handling for Alpaca/data sources
nodes.norm_label: security bin — api-key handling for alpaca/data sources

nodes.label: Alpaca key handling: gitignored alpaca_keys.env, never committed or echoed
nodes.norm_label: alpaca key handling: gitignored alpaca_keys.env, never committed or echoed

nodes.label: Live-trading hard guard (is_live() in Trading's alpaca_client.py; PAPER default, live opt-in)
nodes.norm_label: live-trading hard guard (is_live() in trading's alpaca_client.py; paper default, live opt-in)

nodes.label: Volatility-Compression (TTM Squeeze) Breakout
nodes.rationale: Run only because it is a genuinely NEW family (breakout/volatility-expansion never tested in E1-E7/E3) — novelty is information, not parameter fishing. A-priori prior POOR (program base rate 0/8; breakout systems on liquid indices post-2000 historically weak). Long-only because the short side of US equities is structurally worse for a small account.

nodes.label: Frozen 29-ETF Universe (swing_bot/universe.py, frozen 2026-07-08)
nodes.rationale: Coverage-gated by data_start + warmup; liquidity floor inherited. Staggered inception means the early gate window trades ~20 eligible tickers vs 29 by 2018 — disclosed, not corrected.

nodes.label: Split-Adjusted / Dividend-UNadjusted Data Convention (auto_adjust=False)
nodes.rationale: Program-wide convention: understates long-hold returns slightly but biases against strategy and benchmark equally; consistency across the program is the point.

nodes.label: Hostile-Regime Gate Window 2000-01-01 → 2013-12-31 (CAGR ≥15%, maxDD ≤60%)
nodes.rationale: The hostile regimes (dot-com, 2008) decide the verdict; secondary 2014→end window reported always. Interpretability floor on n_trades converts thin samples to INCONCLUSIVE rather than PASS/FAIL. No parameter may change after seeing any result.

nodes.label: Never-Book-a-Loss Deep-Dip Strategy (buy ≤0.80×ATH, sell +15%, no stop)
nodes.rationale: Audit with pre-stated dual readout: (1) the 'never book a loss' property will be nearly true in realized terms — the seductive statistic; (2) NAV will still show large unrealized drawdown and CAGR well below 15% because capital idles, winners are capped, losers held uncapped. Either prediction failing is informative. ETFs used because a single-stock version is survivorship-poisoned (E3 lesson).
nodes.norm_label: never-book-a-loss deep-dip strategy (buy ≤0.80×ath, sell +15%, no stop)

nodes.label: Post-Earnings-Announcement Drift (PEAD)
nodes.rationale: The one 'real edge' named independently by three practitioner sources; a documented academic anomaly (Ball & Brown 1968), decayed in large caps after ~2010. Single-stock phenomenon, so it inherits E3's survivorship + lookahead problem and its falsification-only framing: only a FAIL is interpretable; a PASS routes to forward live paper. Signal is price-based only so estimate restatements cannot contaminate it.

nodes.label: Five-Source Swing-Trading Article Set (Swing Trading Research.md)
nodes.norm_label: five-source swing-trading article set (swing trading research.md)

nodes.label: RVOL ≥ 1.5 Volume-Confirmation Gate
nodes.rationale: Why E11 is a new experiment, not a retune of failed E8: the RVOL ≥ 1.5 gate is specified a priori by external sources (SMB, CapTrader, ex-Trillium trader), NOT reverse-engineered from E8's losing trades. E8's gates and exits held identical; the ONLY change is the added entry condition. Prior remains POOR.

nodes.label: Confirmed-Capitulation Mean Reversion ('Right Side of the V')
nodes.rationale: Distinct from E1 (IBS) which buys the dip while still falling: E12's novelty is the confirmation gate — volume-climax capitulation PLUS confirmed upside reversal (close > prior day's high) before entry, designed to sidestep the falling-knife problem that partly sank the IBS family. New mechanism → legitimate new experiment, not an IBS retune. close>high(t-1) is the honest EOD proxy for what pros judge intraday.
nodes.norm_label: confirmed-capitulation mean reversion ('right side of the v')

nodes.label: LLM-Council Program Selection (Record Appendix B)
nodes.rationale: Council selected Wave 0 infra (#29/#30/#28), Wave 1 backtest (prereg #1, then #15+#13 fill-timing ablation, #1 with #27 side-check), Wave 2 gated live; deferred 7 ideas, dropped 16.

nodes.label: IBS Mean Reversion on Equity ETFs (E1 family)
nodes.rationale: Ranked build-first: strongest published, replicated EOD evidence (Pagonidis 2013, arXiv 2306.12434, Kinlay), computable from OHLC alone, and trading ETFs sidesteps survivorship bias entirely. Known caveats stated up front: published effects are close-to-close so next-open execution forfeits the overnight component; edge is small per trade so cost modeling is decisive; public signal so expect decay.
nodes.norm_label: ibs mean reversion on equity etfs (e1 family)

nodes.label: Survivorship Bias
nodes.rationale: ETFs mostly eliminate it; stock mean reversion is the worst-affected class since delisted crashers are precisely what oversold triggers buy.

nodes.label: PDT Rule Elimination (FINRA 26-10, effective 2026-06-04)
nodes.rationale: Removes the $25k day-trading minimum, so same-day stop-outs no longer risk regulatory lockout for sub-$25k accounts; Alpaca removes PDT API fields by 2026-07-06, so ported code reading daytrade_count/pattern_day_trader will break.

nodes.label: EOD Execution Model (signal at close, execute next open)
nodes.norm_label: eod execution model (signal at close, execute next open)

nodes.label: Trend-Filtered Pullback (buy weakness in an uptrend)
nodes.rationale: Evan's separate 'trend pullback' and 'mean reversion' candidates folded into ONE strategy family — long-term trend filter + short-term oversold trigger is the only version of either with consistent quantified support.
nodes.norm_label: trend-filtered pullback (buy weakness in an uptrend)

nodes.label: Pagonidis — The IBS Effect: Mean Reversion in Equity ETFs (2013, NAAIM)
nodes.author: Alexander Pagonidis
nodes.norm_label: pagonidis — the ibs effect: mean reversion in equity etfs (2013, naaim)

nodes.label: de Groot, Huij & Zhou — Another Look at Trading Costs and Short-Term Reversal Profits (2011/2012)
nodes.author: de Groot, Huij & Zhou
nodes.norm_label: de groot, huij & zhou — another look at trading costs and short-term reversal profits (2011/2012)

nodes.label: Lo, Mamaysky & Wang — Foundations of Technical Analysis (2000, J. Finance)
nodes.author: Lo, Mamaysky & Wang
nodes.norm_label: lo, mamaysky & wang — foundations of technical analysis (2000, j. finance)

nodes.label: Post-Hoc Universe-Narrowing Prohibition
nodes.rationale: broad_us alone cleared all four criteria but this does NOT rescue E1: the pre-registered experiment was the full 29-ETF universe and it FAILED. Selecting broad_us after seeing results is post-hoc narrowing, forbidden by prereg §10 — it becomes a NEW hypothesis (E1b) under its own dated pre-registration. A lead, not a result.

nodes.label: E1 Fill-Timing Ablation (M1.8, overnight vs next-open)
nodes.norm_label: e1 fill-timing ablation (m1.8, overnight vs next-open)

nodes.label: Overnight Gap Component of the Mean-Reversion Edge
nodes.rationale: Measured: 54% of the close-to-close IBS edge lives in the close→next-open gap (6.3 of 11.8 bps pooled); next-open execution keeps ~64% of the edge because reversion continues into a second night. Many single-country ETFs' IBS edge is a stale-NAV/overnight artifact a next-open loop cannot harvest. This gap between 'the effect is real' and 'you can capture it at next open' became the story of the entire project.
nodes.norm_label: overnight gap component of the mean-reversion edge

nodes.label: E1 Power Calculation (M1.6, signal frequency)
nodes.rationale: E1 is signal-abundant and capacity-constrained (19.6% of bar-days signal; ~1,431 signal-days/yr): the limit is concurrency, not signals. Integrity note: no post-signal returns computed before the pre-registration commit. Honest caveat: frequency ≠ edge.

nodes.label: E1b broad_us Out-of-Sample Results — FAIL by 0.0039 Sharpe
nodes.norm_label: e1b broad_us out-of-sample results — fail by 0.0039 sharpe

nodes.label: Multiplicity Caveat (endless re-pre-registration is p-hacking)
nodes.rationale: A third pre-registration lowering the cost assumption is defensible ONLY on independent liquidity grounds set before the run, and must carry a pre-committed stop (fail → family shelved). Endless re-pre-registration to chase a near-miss is p-hacking with extra steps.

nodes.label: E2 Leveraged-ETF IBS Results — FAIL, IBS Family SHELVED
nodes.norm_label: e2 leveraged-etf ibs results — fail, ibs family shelved

nodes.label: IBS Family Pre-Committed Stop (SHELVED per prereg 865c09e §7)
nodes.rationale: Three pre-registered IBS tests failed (E1, E1b near-miss, E2), so the pre-committed stop executes: no E2b, no E1c, no cost-shaved or execution-shaved re-runs. Only a NEW dated decision by Evan re-opens the family; the executing model will not.
nodes.norm_label: ibs family pre-committed stop (shelved per prereg 865c09e §7)

nodes.label: Fixed-Dollar Sizing Engine Flaw (K=1 NAV went negative)
nodes.rationale: The engine sizes at fixed initial-capital/K dollars, not current-NAV/K — after losses it keeps buying full size (implicit leverage); at K=1 in 3× funds NAV crossed zero (104% maxDD). Immaterial for E1/E1b, does not change the E2 gate verdict; recorded as a known limitation, not silently fixed post-hoc. Any future engine must size on current NAV.
nodes.norm_label: fixed-dollar sizing engine flaw (k=1 nav went negative)

nodes.label: E4 200d-MA Leverage Rotation Results — PASS (33.76% CAGR)
nodes.norm_label: e4 200d-ma leverage rotation results — pass (33.76% cagr)

nodes.label: 200-Day MA Leverage Rotation (QQQ signal → hold TQQQ / cash)
nodes.rationale: Value is drawdown reduction, not return enhancement: it trades ~4.6pp CAGR vs buy-hold TQQQ to cut peak drawdown 82%→58%. Survives costs structurally (~4 switches/year) where the IBS family died on per-trade costs.
nodes.norm_label: 200-day ma leverage rotation (qqq signal → hold tqqq / cash)

nodes.label: Regime Flattery (leverage × a secular bull)
nodes.rationale: 3× Nasdaq over 2014–2026 is leverage on the best tech decade in history; the base engine is 'leverage × a secular bull'. Discount forward expectations hard — the +2.45%/mo is a backtest ceiling, not a forecast. This flag directly motivated E5's hostile-regime test.

nodes.label: E5 Hostile-Regime Test Results — FAIL (E4 de-authorized, 92.7% DD)
nodes.norm_label: e5 hostile-regime test results — fail (e4 de-authorized, 92.7% dd)

nodes.label: Synthetic 3× Fund Validation (drag-calibrated, corr 0.9989 to real TQQQ)
nodes.rationale: Synthetic daily-rebalanced 3× Nasdaq with 4.00%/yr calibrated drag reproduces real TQQQ to 0.05pp CAGR — so extending the test to 2000–2013 (before TQQQ existed) is a legitimate test, not an artifact.
nodes.norm_label: synthetic 3× fund validation (drag-calibrated, corr 0.9989 to real tqqq)

nodes.label: Whipsaw Failure Mode in Secular Bear Markets
nodes.rationale: In choppy secular bears (2000–2002, 2008) violent counter-trend rallies push price back above the 200-day MA, the strategy re-enters 3× leverage, and the next leg down destroys it — repeatedly. The 200-MA did not protect: 92.7% drawdown 2000–2013.
nodes.norm_label: whipsaw failure mode in secular bear markets

nodes.label: E6 De-Leveraged 1× Rotation Results — PASS (first robust regime-spanning result)
nodes.norm_label: e6 de-leveraged 1× rotation results — pass (first robust regime-spanning result)

nodes.label: 1× 200-MA Drawdown-Management Overlay
nodes.rationale: Real, robust effect: improves Sharpe in every regime and roughly halves worst drawdown (83%→52%) by sitting in cash through crashes. But NOT the high-return goal — full-period CAGR 8.04% vs QQQ's 7.92%; the value is almost entirely drawdown reduction. 2× is not worth it (Sharpe below 1×, 80% DD back); the sweet spot is 1×.

nodes.label: Experiment Catalog v2 (grounded in measured E1/E1b/E2 data)
nodes.rationale: Every prior labeled MEASURED, LITERATURE, or TBD — no invented figures. Exploratory screens are hypothesis-GENERATING; the chosen candidate is then confirmed under its own prereg.
nodes.norm_label: experiment catalog v2 (grounded in measured e1/e1b/e2 data)

nodes.label: Cost-Fragility Constraint (edges under ~15bps gross die on costs)
nodes.norm_label: cost-fragility constraint (edges under ~15bps gross die on costs)

nodes.label: Out-of-Sample Decay Constraint (train→holdout roughly halves returns)
nodes.norm_label: out-of-sample decay constraint (train→holdout roughly halves returns)

nodes.label: NAV-Proportional Sizing Engine v2 (C1)
nodes.rationale: Prerequisite for any future experiment — invalid fixed-dollar sizing poisons everything after; frozen refs re-pinned as v2 alongside v1.

nodes.label: Gayed — Leverage for the Long Run (2016 Dow Award)
nodes.author: Michael Gayed
nodes.norm_label: gayed — leverage for the long run (2016 dow award)

nodes.label: A3/B1/B4 In-Sample Screen Results (hypothesis-generating)
nodes.rationale: A3 (overnight-only IBS harvest): the overnight component is real but not separately harvestable at retail cost. B1 (gap-down reversion at open): hypothesis largely refuted. B4 (TQQQ/QQQ 200d-MA rotation): the standout by an order of magnitude → recommended pre-registering E4.

nodes.label: B4 Vol-Regime Leverage Rotation Screen (TQQQ/QQQ, +2.15%/mo holdout)
nodes.norm_label: b4 vol-regime leverage rotation screen (tqqq/qqq, +2.15%/mo holdout)

nodes.label: Holdout Contamination (screens SAW 2022–2026)
nodes.rationale: Because the screens looked at 2022–2026, a follow-up prereg cannot claim unseen-holdout purity on this data; confirmation must come from a pre-registered robustness battery plus live paper as the only true OOS. This is why E4's gate ran on the full window with the battery as the un-peeked evidence.

nodes.label: E10+E11+E12 Results — All FAIL (article-set arc)
nodes.rationale: E10 nuance: PEAD is the only experiment in the program to beat both benchmarks in the hostile 2000–2013 regime (+5.93%/yr while its universe lost money), but decayed post-2014 — 'real but small and decayed', still a decisive FAIL for high return. E12's instructive mechanism: waiting for confirmation did WORSE than raw dip-buying because the confirmation bar surrenders exactly the reversal pop that holds the edge — the same overnight-gap arithmetic that killed the IBS family.

nodes.label: Program Falsification Conclusion (0 PASS / 13 pre-registered attempts / 6 families)
nodes.rationale: Every codifiable strategy from both the Reddit thread and the five-article set is tested and falsified under pre-registration: no robust high-return EOD edge in anything tested at retail scale. Families: index mean reversion, leveraged trend, stock momentum, breakout, deep-dip accumulation, event-driven/PEAD.
nodes.norm_label: program falsification conclusion (0 pass / 13 pre-registered attempts / 6 families)

nodes.label: E3 Concentrated Stock Momentum Results — FAIL (clean)
nodes.rationale: Failed the 15% return bar by a wide margin (6.27%) WITH survivorship + lookahead bias helping it, and momentum selection underperformed equal-weight buy-hold of its own universe in every window — the selection itself destroyed value. Closes the third and last plausible route (after mean reversion and leveraged trend) to a high-return executable retail edge.
nodes.norm_label: e3 concentrated stock momentum results — fail (clean)

nodes.label: Asymmetric-Falsification Framing (only a FAIL is interpretable)
nodes.rationale: On a survivorship- and lookahead-biased universe the biases can only INFLATE results, so a FAIL is clean and interpretable while a PASS would be uninterpretable (it would route to forward live paper instead). Inherited by E10.
nodes.norm_label: asymmetric-falsification framing (only a fail is interpretable)

nodes.label: E7 International Validation Results — BOTH ARMS FAIL
nodes.rationale: The clean close: the one credible untested high-return idea (a-priori vol-gated 3× rotation) was tested on five genuinely unseen non-US regimes with knobs fixed before seeing data and failed every gate. Downgrades E6 too: the 1× overlay is real but market-dependent (works in Japan/Germany/HK/US, hurts risk-adjusted return in UK/Australia) — a useful-in-some-regimes tool, not a universal law.
nodes.norm_label: e7 international validation results — both arms fail

nodes.label: 3× Leverage Single-Day Wipeout Tail Risk (1987 Hang Seng)
nodes.rationale: The 1987 Hang Seng crash (>33% in one day) drives any 3× daily-rebalanced fund to zero permanently; no timing overlay can recover from a single-day wipeout. A mathematical, not statistical, argument against extreme leverage.
nodes.norm_label: 3× leverage single-day wipeout tail risk (1987 hang seng)

nodes.label: E8+E9 Results — Both FAIL (squeeze breakout; never-book-a-loss audit)
nodes.rationale: E8: compression predicts expansion, not direction — 31–38% win rate with no tail winners large enough to carry it. E9: both pre-registered predictions confirmed — 100% realized win rate (0 losses in 53 trades) coexisting with 3.46% gate CAGR, a −79.7% unrealized position, a 17-year underwater hold, and 38% dead capital; the win-rate statistic measures bookkeeping, not performance.
nodes.norm_label: e8+e9 results — both fail (squeeze breakout; never-book-a-loss audit)

nodes.label: Frozen Regression Tripwire (12 refs, d=±0.0000pp)
nodes.rationale: Pinned reference numbers verified GREEN after each run prove swing.db and the engine were untouched, making results tamper-evident.

Falsification Program — 13 pre-registered attempts, 6 families, 0 passes
